Nazi Looted Booksat Penn Libraries
Markings guideCasesRegisterSourcesAbout
/

The Penn Libraries hold roughly two thousand one hundred books taken from people the Nazis persecuted and murdered. This site is a record of what we have identified — and, just as plainly, of what we still cannot account for.

2,100+
identified in the collection
2,391–5,549
believed sent here after the war

We have not closed the distance between these two figures. Keeping it visible is the point — read what we cannot account for.

01Identify looted books in your own collectionA visual guide to every marking these books carry, and where in the book they hide.→02How these books were foundA fifteen-year search, told through the cases that turned on a single stamp.→03What Penn holdsBrowse the full register, ordered by the strength of the evidence.→
Scope of this recordThe method, as a manualSources & databasesDownload the data
Penn Libraries · Provenance of looted books · prototypeUncertainty in this record is shown, not hidden.
